items.title: Stablecoin flows and spillovers to FX markets
items.desc: by Iñaki Aldasoro, Paula Beltrán, Federico GrinbergUsing data on four USD-pegged stablecoins and 27 fiat currencies, this paper documents spillovers from stablecoin-based foreign exchange (FX) to traditional FXmarkets. We document a gap between the cost of acquiring dollars via stablecoins and via the spot FX market (parity deviations). To establish a causal link between stablecoin flows and FX markets, we use a granular instrumental variable that exploits idiosyncratic shocks to stablecoin net inflows in other currencies.

items.title: How the House Financial Services Committee is taking on tokenization: State of Crypto
items.desc: Rep. French Hill, head of the House Financial Services Committee, weighed in on outstanding policy issues in an interview with CoinDesk last month.

items.title: Kraken ‘plans’ regulated perp contracts as rivals are quick off the blocks
items.desc: In the wake of CFTC approval of perpetual futures contracts tied to the spot price of Bitcoin, Kraken expects to offer those to US institutional clients within "the next month."

items.title: Bitcoin’s biggest quantum risk may not be wallet keys. An early investor fears something bigger
items.desc: Andrew Gault, the venture capitalist who funded the quantum hardware labs now threatening bitcoin, says the industry is looking in the wrong place. Google's own security team moved in the same direction in March.

items.title: How Stellar became part of DTCC's tokenization push for Wall Street securities onchain
items.desc: Wall Street's clearing giant chose a public blockchain with compliance tools built for regulated assets, Stellar Development Foundation CEO Denelle Dixon said.

items.title: Bitcoin preps 3% May downside, but US PMI data may boost BTC price
items.desc: Bitcoin faced ending May lower by around 3%, with the possibility of US PMI data giving BTC price action a boost next week.

items.title: Cardano Foundation cancels 2026 summit after treasury funding vote falls just short
items.desc: The measure needed two-thirds support but got only 65%, even after late endorsements from Cardano's founder and the Foundation CEO.

items.title: The institutional edge: moomoo targets Wall Street-grade trading tools for retail crypto investors
items.desc: The brokerage giant says the future of investing won’t be defined by asset access alone but by the quality of tools investors get.

items.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
items.desc: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, Web3 and crypto regulation.

items.title: Argentine Prosecutors Arrest 24, Seize Over $8M in USDT in 'Fake Coins' Crypto-Fraud Sweep
items.desc: The Buenos Aires Public Prosecutor's Office said it ran 90 simultaneous raids and froze more than 8 million USDT across three fraud rings — fake trading apps, WhatsApp hijacks, and a Chinese-origin infostealer operation — in what it calls the province's largest crypto seizure to date.

items.title: Bitcoin's wild days are over — and Trace Mayer says that's a good thing
items.desc: The creator of the Mayer Multiple argues bitcoin’s growing economic substance is compressing volatility and attracting deeper capital.

items.title: Alephium Bridge Loses $815K to Forged Guardian Messages, Not Stolen Keys
items.desc: Alephium's Wormhole-fork TokenBridge was drained on Ethereum and BNB Chain in roughly seven minutes after an off-chain backend flaw let fraudulent messages slip past its four-guardian network, the team said in a public correction.

items.title: Vietnam proposes allowing SMEs to use digital assets as loan collateral
items.desc: Vietnam’s Ministry of Finance has proposed letting SMEs use digital assets, virtual assets and intellectual property as loan collateral.

items.title: Cosmos-based Gravity Bridge halts bridge after reported $5.4M exploit
items.desc: Gravity Bridge was drained of roughly $5.4 million in a suspected signing key compromise, prompting validators to halt the bridge while an investigation is underway.

items.title: Bitcoin sentiment reaches most ‘lopsided positive’ ratio for 2026: Santiment
items.desc: Crypto sentiment platform Santiment warned that the two biggest spikes in social media bullishness around Bitcoin have previously been followed by short-term price pullbacks.

items.title: Bitcoin dip buyers curb selling but questionable spot, futures volumes highlight weakness
items.desc: Bitcoin dip buyers are present near range lows, and new leveraged longs opened in the zone but the volumes lack the size needed to reverse the downtrend.

items.title: XRP Ledger's new proposal blocks the flash loan attacks costing DeFi hundreds of millions
items.desc: A draft XRPL amendment notes that flash loan attacks are "structurally impossible" on the network because of how its transactions are built, an architectural quirk that has spared the chain from the exploit class that has cost Ethereum DeFi billions.

items.title: Bitcoin is at ‘pivotal level’ as $65K downside risk looms: Analyst
items.desc: While Bitcoin is hovering around $73,000, a crypto trader says the current setup is “different from the previous breakdown in February.”

items.title: Polymarket Plugs Into OneFootball's 645M-Fan Network Two Weeks Before the World Cup
items.desc: The exclusive deal puts prediction markets in front of OneFootball's 200 million monthly active users — and a wider 645-million-fan ecosystem — as Polymarket's 2026 soccer-distribution stack reaches saturation ahead of the June kickoff.

items.title: Senator Lummis says China will 'write the rules' of the new financial era if CLARITY fails
items.desc: The Senate Banking Committee voted to advance the CLARITY Act in May, but it must still pass both chambers of Congress before heading to the president's desk.

items.title: Circle freezes $12.6M of USDC linked to privacy protocol Zama
items.desc: The $12.6 million in USDC was likely frozen in connection with an ongoing but unrelated civil court case, according to onchain sleuth ZachXBT.

items.title: Cosmos-based Gravity Bridge drained of $5.4 million in suspected key compromise, researchers say
items.desc: The attacker took USDC, ether, tether and PAYG tokens before laundering a portion through ChangeNow and Binance.

items.title: Circle Freeze on Zama's Confidential USDC Locks $12.6M of User Funds in DeFi 'Crossfire'
items.desc: A court-ordered restraining order pushed Circle to blacklist Zama's cUSDC wrapper on Ethereum. The privacy protocol is not a defendant but every depositor in the pooled contract is locked out alongside the targeted funds.

items.title: U.S. says it seized about $1 billion in Iranian crypto as pressure campaign expands
items.desc: The seizure, falling under "Operation Economic Fury," aims to restrict Iran's access to overseas revenue, banking networks, and crypto infrastructure.

items.title: Florida Candidate Liquidates $800K in Bitcoin to Bankroll Congressional Bid
items.desc: A Republican candidate jockeying to represent Florida’s 22nd Congressional District liquidated Bitcoin to help fuel his political bid.

items.title: When the market is bad, we build: Inside Binance’s bold 2030 master plan
items.desc: Established crypto firms will merge with traditional finance, but neither Wall Street bankers nor corporate giants will take over the crypto industry, said Binance’s Head of VIP and Institutional, Catherine Chen.

items.title: Hyperliquid could become a ‘financial services juggernaut’ as DeFi expands, says Grayscale
items.desc: The digital asset manager says Hyperliquid is emerging as a blockchain-based financial infrastructure platform with the potential to expand beyond crypto trading and challenge parts of traditional derivatives and exchange markets.

items.title: Court-ordered Circle freeze traps $12.6 million in Zama cUSDC contract amid Overnight Finance suit
items.desc: Zama CEO Rand Hindi said the protocol's confidential USDC contract was "caught in a crossfire" and that his team is investigating the freeze.

items.title: XRP ETFs add $35 million as bitcoin and ether funds lost $2 billion in late May
items.desc: From May 20 to May 29, XRP funds took in $35 million while bitcoin and ether ETFs lost roughly $2 billion combined, with Ripple’s earlier reported XRP treasury plan still awaiting confirmation.

items.title: Crypto treasuries turn to risky equity in drive to raise cash
items.desc: Companies that stock up on crypto assets are following Michael Saylor’s Strategy into so-called digital credit

items.title: Spot Bitcoin ETFs see record 10-day outflow streak, analyst calls it ‘contrarian indicator’
items.desc: Spot Bitcoin ETFs logged a record 10-day outflow streak totaling nearly $3 billion, while Ether ETFs bled for 14 consecutive sessions.

items.title: Bitcoin treasury space still has fair share of ‘carnival barkers’: BSTR founder
items.desc: BSTR co-founder Sean Bill says many Bitcoin treasury companies lack the “ability to actually deploy Bitcoin.”

items.title: Bitcoin retail sentiment still matters, says Swan Bitcoin CEO
items.desc: Swan Bitcoin CEO Cory Klippsten said it is important to pay attention to retail sentiment around Bitcoin, as ownership is still far from concentrated and “it’s not like Blackrock owns the Bitcoin.”

items.title: US agrees ‘perpetual’ futures trading after offshore Hyperliquid’s huge growth
items.desc: Oil-linked trading on unregulated and decentralised crypto exchange has skyrocketed during Iran war

items.title: CME Group Launches 24/7 Crypto Futures and Options Trading
items.desc: CME Group announced it has expanded its crypto derivatives offering to around-the-clock trading, allowing traders to access Bitcoin and Ethereum futures and options whenever they choose.

items.title: Bitcoin dip buyers place $500M in bids as $70K retest looms
items.desc: Over $500 million in Bitcoin bids now sit near $70,000, while options traders and futures positions converge around the same level.

items.title: Jamie Dimon Vows JPMorganChase Will Fight Clarity Act
items.desc: JPMorganChase is going to fight the Clarity Act, Chairman and CEO Jamie Dimon said Friday (May 29). Speaking on the Fox Business show “Mornings with Maria,” Dimon said that if crypto companies like Coinbase are going to take deposits like a bank, they should be regulated like a bank and should be required to follow anti-money laundering (AML), […] The post Jamie Dimon Vows JPMorganChase Will Fight Clarity Act appeared first on PYMNTS.com.

items.title: ‘Extraordinarily unusual’ for CFTC to reverse Gemini settlement deal: Ex-chair
items.desc: As the CFTC and Gemini work together to seek a court's reversal of a 2025 settlement, one of the agency’s former chairs said the public “deserves a better explanation.”

items.title: Coinkite Launches Coldcard MK5: Major UX Upgrades to Flagship Bitcoin Hardware Wallet
items.desc: Bitcoin Magazine Coinkite Launches Coldcard MK5: Major UX Upgrades to Flagship Bitcoin Hardware Wallet Coinkite has released the Coldcard MK5, building on the MK4’s dual secure element security with a larger Gorilla Glass screen, redesigned buttons, and improved NFC capabilities for smoother Bitcoin transactions. This post Coinkite Launches Coldcard MK5: Major UX Upgrades to Flagship Bitcoin Hardware Wallet first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Juan Galt.

items.title: 'He’s Full of Shit': JP Morgan's Jamie Dimon Takes Aim at Coinbase CEO Over Clarity Act
items.desc: Dimon vowed to fight the passage of the crypto market structure bill until the bitter end.

items.title: Celsius Founder Alex Mashinsky Files to Have 12-Year Crypto Fraud Sentence Vacated
items.desc: Celsius founder and former CEO Alex Mashinsky hopes to have his prison sentence vacated, claiming a legal conflict tied to Sam Bankman-Fried.

items.title: ‘The banks will not accept it’: Dimon escalates battle over stablecoin rewards in CLARITY Act debate
items.desc: JPMorgan CEO Jamie Dimon criticized Coinbase CEO Brian Armstrong and warned the current CLARITY Act framework could ultimately fail, as banks and crypto firms clash over whether stablecoin issuers should be allowed to offer yield-bearing rewards that resemble bank deposits.

items.title: U.S. Treasury: The United States Has Seized Nearly $1 Billion of Iran’s Crypto
items.desc: Bitcoin Magazine U.S. Treasury: The United States Has Seized Nearly $1 Billion of Iran’s Crypto Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said the U.S. has seized up to $1 billion in Iran-linked crypto. This post U.S. Treasury: The United States Has Seized Nearly $1 Billion of Iran’s Crypto first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.

items.title: Coinbase Becomes First US Exchange Allowed to Offer Global Crypto Perps Trading
items.desc: Coinbase can offer U.S. customers access to offshore crypto perpetual futures, a risky form of leveraged crypto trading, the CFTC said Friday.

items.title: CVE-2024-52911 - Script Interpreter Remote Crash
items.desc: After Bitcoin Core 0.14.0 and before Bitcoin Core 29.0, validating a specially-crafted block may cause the node to access previously freed memory. During validation, necessary data required for checking inputs for each transaction is pre-calculated and cached. For specially crafted invalid blocks, it was possible for this data to be destroyed while it was still being accessed by a background validation thread. An attacker capable of mining a block with sufficient proof-of-work could have exploited this to crash victim nodes. Because of the nature of use-after-free bugs, it is possible that the crash could have been used for remote code execution, though constraints on the input (block) data make this unlikely. This issue is considered High severity. Details By default, script validation for new blocks is dispatched to background threads via a vector of CScriptCheck functors. Each CScriptCheck holds a pointer to a PrecomputedTransactionData object which stores some data needed by each input in the transaction. Because it stores a pointer and not the data itself, care must be taken to ensure that the PrecomputedTransactionData outlives the CScriptCheck. The script checks lifetime is enforced by an RAII class, CCheckQueueControl. However, the control is intantiated before the precomputed transaction data. Because local objects in C++ are destructed in reverse order of construction, this means the vector of PrecomputedTransactionData is destroyed before the CCheckQueueControl. This is not an issue when the block is valid, as CCheckQueueControl::Wait() will be called before the function returns and the PrecomputedTransactionData gets destroyed. However, in case of an early return (when a separate check fails) a background script thread may read the precomputed transaction data after it was destroyed. An attacker could exploit this to crash victim nodes at the expense of a valid PoW at tip. Attribution Cory Fields (MIT DCI) discovered this vulnerability and responsibly disclosed it in a detailed report containing a proof of concept for reproduction and a proposed mitigation. Timeline 2024-11-02 Cory Fields privately reports the bug 2024-11-06 Pieter Wuille pushes a covert fix to already open PR #31112 which works around the issue by removing the early returns 2024-12-03 PR #31112 is merged 2025-04-12 Bitcoin Core version 29.0 is released with a fix 2026-04-19 The last vulnerable Bitcoin Core version (28.x) goes end of life 2026-05-05 Public disclosure.
